location_precision.why: Every finding carries a usable file:line, e.g. 'src/sync.py:14' and 'src/report.py:11'.

interaction_synthesis.why: Each defect is listed in isolation with no compound failure mode explained across defects (e.g. the swallowed errors at :39 are never linked to persisting bad state from another defect).

causal_clarity.why: Essentially every finding states a mechanism, e.g. mutable default 'seen=[]' is 'shared across calls, so IDs synced in one invocation are wrongly skipped on every later invocation' and the report early return 'exits before f.close()'.

location_precision.why: Every one of the six findings carries a usable file:line (e.g. "src/sync.py:14", "src/report.py:11").

interaction_synthesis.why: Each finding is described in isolation with no compound failure mode connecting defects (e.g. the swallowed errors at sync.py:39 are never linked to any resulting bad state elsewhere).

causal_clarity.why: Every finding states a mechanism, such as "Mutable default argument `seen=[]` is shared across calls, so IDs synced in one invocation are wrongly skipped on every later invocation."

location_precision.why: Every one of the six findings carries a usable file:line, e.g. 'src/sync.py:14' and 'src/report.py:11'.

interaction_synthesis.why: The findings are listed as independent defects with no statement of how any two combine into a compound failure mode (e.g. the swallowed error at :39 is never linked to a persisting bad state elsewhere).

causal_clarity.why: Every finding states a failure mechanism or breaking condition, e.g. 'Mutable default argument seen=[] is shared across calls, so IDs synced in one invocation are wrongly skipped on every later invocation.'
